title: Fix CSS Grid Layout with Inconsistent Row Height Calculation

statement.markdown: Given a CSS grid layout with dynamic row heights, write a function to calculate and return the correct row heights. The grid layout is represented as a 2D array where each inner array represents a row and contains the column widths. The function should handle grids with varying row heights and return the correct row heights for the given grid layout.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a 2D array where each inner array represents a row and contains the column widths.
statement.outputFormat: Output is an array representing the correct row heights.
statement.notes: The grid layout can have varying row heights and the function should handle this.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the grid layout
Number of columns in the grid layout

examples.explanation: The function should return the correct row heights for the given grid layout
The function should return the correct row heights for the given grid layout

hints.text: First hint text: The function should iterate through each row in the grid layout and calculate the maximum height of the row.
Second hint text: The function should use the maximum height of the row as the row height and return the array of row heights.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: The function should iterate through each row in the grid layout and calculate the maximum height of the row.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: The function should use a nested loop to calculate the maximum height of each row.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: The function should use a single loop to iterate through each row and calculate the maximum height.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The function should return the correct row heights for the given grid layout.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: The function should return the correct row heights for the given grid layout.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: The function should not use a nested loop to calculate the maximum height of each row.
Mistake 2: The function should not return the array of column widths as the row heights.
